stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI domains including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The pipeline indicates its primary use case is 'text-generation'.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's Llama family of models.

stories.title: Nvidia, Microsoft, and Arm are all teasing Nvidia’s new N1X laptop processors
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Nvidia is reportedly preparing to announce its own custom Arm-powered laptop processors at Computex.
The announcement is being heavily teased by Nvidia, Microsoft, and Arm across social media platforms.
This signals a significant industry shift towards integrated, potentially AI-optimized, Arm architectures in laptops.

stories.title: SpaceX gets $4 billion contract to build missile-tracking ‘Golden Dome’ satellites
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: SpaceX secured a $4.16 billion contract from the Pentagon to develop missile-tracking satellites.
These new sensor-equipped satellites will enable the US Space Force to detect and track targets from space.
The system is linked to President Trump's proposed 'Golden Dome' defense infrastructure.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen is a novel LLM designed to generate editable Printed Circuit Board (PCB) schematics directly from natural language requests.
The research addresses a critical bottleneck in hardware design: the manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB schematic creation.
The paper tackles the challenges of creating LLM-suited representations and acquiring large-scale datasets for this domain.

stories.title: GPIC: A Giant Permissive Image Corpus for Visual Generation
stories.author: Li Fei-Fei
bullet_points: Introduction of GPIC, a massive, permissively licensed image corpus containing approximately 28 trillion pixels.
The dataset is curated with captions generated by a state-of-the-art vision-language model, featuring 100M training examples.
GPIC is designed to address the need for large, accessible, and stable datasets for scalable visual generative modeling.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
The model is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors.
It is associated with the DeepSeek_v3 architecture, indicating relevance in the LLM space.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon devices, optimizing AI workloads for the ecosystem.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents that run entirely on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are a collaboration involving ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools).
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon introduces Data Mixture Surgery (DMS) to analyze the hidden pretraining data composition of LLMs.
The research addresses the lack of transparency regarding LLM 'digital DNA' (training data mixture), which dictates model behavior.
The tool allows estimation of the domain-level distribution of an LLM's corpus using only its generated text.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in LLM reasoning where intermediate steps are tied to external, autoregressive token generation.
The proposed method aims to mimic human cognition by enabling LLMs to use internal 'working memory' for information manipulation.
This approach seeks to decouple internal computational reasoning from the external process of generating output tokens.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global correctness.
Introduces a formal measure, the 'compositional residual eps*', to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
Provides a runtime computable method to bound the deviation from a theoretically coherent state based on system output and constraints.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data mixture of Large Language Models (LLMs), which is crucial to their behavior.
The core contribution is Data Mixture Surgery (DMS), a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus using only its generated text.
This work enables post-hoc auditing and understanding of an LLM's 'digital DNA' without direct access to its training data.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, a novel large language model designed to generate editable PCB schematics.
Addresses the critical gap in using generative AI for PCB design, which is currently manual and expertise-intensive.
Overcomes challenges related to the lack of LLM-suited representations and large-scale datasets for this domain.

stories.title: Efficient Test-Time Finetuning of LLMs via Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching
stories.author: Alaa Maalouf
bullet_points: Introduces HullFT, a new method to make Test-Time Finetuning (TTFT) of LLMs faster.
Addresses the bottleneck of TTFT, which currently suffers from slow per-query selection and finetuning.
HullFT aims to balance the trade-off between speed and quality in prompt adaptation for LLMs.

stories.title: SoundnessBench: Can Your AI Scientist Really Tell Good Research Ideas from Bad Ones?
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: Introduces SoundnessBench, a new benchmark designed to test an AI's ability to judge the methodological viability of ML research ideas.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in autonomous AI research agents: the ability to filter poor research proposals before resource expenditure.
The benchmark is constructed from 1,099 ML research proposals originally submitted to ICLR.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates its primary function is text generation.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (e.g., Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has expanded its capabilities to support the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
The update facilitates the use of powerful proprietary models alongside open-source alternatives.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code locally.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Tech companies desperately want to film you doing chores
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: An AI training startup, Shift, is offering free home cleaning services in exchange for user footage.
The service is initially targeting New York City with plans for expansion to cities like London.
The core mechanism involves using real-world domestic activity footage to train AI models.

stories.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A large-scale botnet, comprising over 17 million devices, has been successfully dismantled.
The infrastructure supporting the botnet was reportedly linked to a Russia-based residential proxy network.
This represents a significant cybersecurity operation targeting distributed malicious networks.

stories.title: Physics Is All You Need? A Case Study in Physicist-Supervised AI Development of Scientific Software
stories.author: Nhat-Minh Nguyen
bullet_points: The study investigates the role of AI agents (Claude models) in scientific software development, specifically building a differentiable perturbation theory module (CLAX-PT) in JAX.
It provides a quantified case study ($N=1$) detailing 15 supervision events over 12 days, classifying the level of human intervention required.
The findings show the AI agent achieved significant autonomy, resolving ten tasks independently through iterative testing, supplemented by physicist domain knowledge.

stories.title: VideoMLA: Low-Rank Latent KV Cache for Minute-Scale Autoregressive Video Diffusion
stories.author: Pinar Yanardag
bullet_points: Introduces VideoMLA, a novel approach for video diffusion models.
Addresses memory and latency issues in long-rollout causal video diffusion by modifying the KV cache structure.
Replaces per-head keys and values with a shared low-rank latent attention mechanism (MLA).

stories.title: Tiny but Trusted: Efficient Vision-Language Reasoning for Time-Series Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Ismini Lourentzou
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in detecting anomalies in sequential (time-series) data.
Highlights the gap in current benchmarks, which lack natural-language rationales for anomaly detection, hindering VLM interpretability.
Introduces VisAnomBench, a new benchmark designed to facilitate the development of grounded and interpretable VLM-based anomaly detection.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new technique, using a 'power distribution' derived from base models, can elicit comparable reasoning capabilities to models trained with reinforcement learning.
This method bypasses the need for extensive post-training, curated datasets, or external verifiers.
The primary challenge for practical application is developing an efficient sampler capable of accurately drawing from this specialized power distribution.

stories.title: DynaFLIP: Rethinking Robotics Perception via Tri-Modal-Dynamics Guided Representation
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: DynaFLIP is a new dynamics-aware multimodal pre-training framework designed to improve robot perception.
It addresses a limitation in current robotics pipelines where motion understanding is often left to downstream policies, relying on static visual encoders.
The framework integrates image, language, and 3D flow triplets to push motion understanding upstream into the perception stage.

stories.title: Fairness-Aware Federated Learning with Trajectory Shapley Value
stories.author: Ziqi Wang
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in Federated Learning (FL) regarding fairness and stability due to fixed aggregation weights.
It introduces the Trajectory Shapley Value (TSV) to accurately reflect unequal and time-varying contributions from participating clients.
FL itself is highlighted as a crucial distributed paradigm for training models on privacy-sensitive and heterogeneous data.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new method proposes achieving strong reasoning in language models by sampling from a 'power distribution' derived from the base model.
This technique offers a potential alternative to traditional reinforcement learning fine-tuning, avoiding extra training or curated datasets.
The primary technical hurdle identified is the need for an efficient sampler capable of accurately mixing to this specialized power distribution.

stories.title: In-Context Reward Adaptation for Robust Preference Modeling
stories.author: Ermin Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses a key limitation of RLHF: the reliance on static reward models which struggle with diverse human preferences.
It proposes 'In-Context Reward Adaptation' to allow models to adapt to unseen preference domains without extensive retraining.
This research aims to improve the robustness and generalization capabilities of LLMs when aligning them with heterogeneous human values.

stories.title: Show HN: Tiny-vLLM – high performance LLM inference engine in C++ and CUDA
stories.author: yu3zhou4
bullet_points: Introduction of Tiny-vLLM, a new LLM inference engine.
The engine is implemented in C++ and utilizes CUDA for performance.
Focus is on achieving high performance for LLM inference.

stories.title: VS Code Prompt Files - Custom Slash Commands for GitHub Copilot
stories.author: petermilovcik
bullet_points: Introduces 'Prompt Files' for VS Code integration with GitHub Copilot.
Allows users to convert frequently used Copilot chat requests into custom slash commands.
Enhances developer workflow efficiency by automating repetitive AI interactions.

stories.title: How Model Distillation Actually Works (and What the 'China Distilled Our Model' Headlines Really Mean)
stories.author: p0rt
bullet_points: Provides a practical, non-hype explanation of knowledge distillation in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Differentiates the mechanics of distillation, specifically addressing the implications of distilling from closed APIs.
Analyzes the context and veracity of recent industry claims regarding model distillation (e.g., OpenAI/Anthropic vs. DeepSeek allegations).

stories.title: even the CEOs are walking it back
stories.author: pvgomes
bullet_points: Prominent AI leaders (Sam Altman, Dario Amodei) have retracted previous predictions regarding mass AI-driven job displacement.
The article focuses on the implications of this shift for the software engineering profession.
The content suggests a moderation or revision of the 'AI job apocalypse' narrative.
